name: code-review
goal: Produce a thorough code review of a pull request, including security, performance, readability, and testability, then suggest specific improvements with code snippets.
constraints: Focus on factual issues: bugs, security, performance, maintainability.
Do not comment on style preferences unless they violate PEP 8.
Every suggestion must include a concrete code snippet showing the fix.
Classify issues as: critical / warning / suggestion.
If any critical issue is found, the final verdict must be REJECT.

steps.title: Parse PR metadata and changed files
steps.objective: Extract PR title, description, file list, and diff summary from the input.
steps.prompt: Given the PR description and diff, list: (1) files changed, (2) rough scope (feature/fix/refactor), (3) areas of concern (auth, DB, API, UI).
acceptance: At least 1 area of concern is named or 'none' with justification

steps.title: Security review
steps.objective: Check for SQL injection, XSS, auth bypass, secrets in code, unsafe deserialization, path traversal.
steps.prompt: Review the diff for security vulnerabilities. For each file, check: (1) user input handling, (2) auth/authz, (3) secrets, (4) injection, (5) unsafe imports.
acceptance: Every file has been checked against the 5 categories
Each finding includes file:line reference and severity

steps.title: Code quality and logic review
steps.objective: Check for bugs, race conditions, error handling gaps, type safety, and architectural concerns.
steps.prompt: Review the diff for logic errors, missing error handling, type safety issues, and design problems.
acceptance: Logic issues are identified with reproduction scenarios
Design concerns reference specific SOLID/DRY violations if applicable

steps.title: Final verdict and summary
steps.objective: Synthesize all findings into a structured verdict with clear accept/reject decision.
steps.prompt: Compile all findings. For each: severity, file:line, description, suggested fix (code snippet). Provide final verdict: APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / REJECT.
acceptance: All findings from prior steps are included
If REJECT: critical issue summary is highlighted
